roles.description:
Meta Superintelligence Labs is seeking a Staff Research Engineer to provide technical leadership for the team working on the next generation of AI Assistants powered by frontier\-scale foundation models. This is a high\-impact Individual Contributor (IC) role focused on architecting cutting\-edge measurement and evaluation paradigms that align fast\-moving model development with real end\-user value.As a Staff Research Engineer, you will define the scientific "North Star" behind the evaluation flywheel, drive technical success across methodologies, and partner with product, engineering, and model training teams to steer the system toward reliability and trustworthiness at scale. You will act as a technical anchor, influencing the roadmap through scientific rigor and hands\-on contribution.  

  

  

### **Staff Research Engineer, MetaAI Assistant Measurement Responsibilities:**

* Architect Scientific Strategy: Define and lead the execution of the scientific roadmap for AI Assistant measurement, ensuring methodologies are rigorous, scalable, and aligned with product goals
* Innovate \& Build: Spearhead the research and development of novel offline and online evaluation metrics, automated benchmarks, and synthetic data generation pipelines to close the loop between model training and deployment
* Cross\-Functional Technical Leadership: Serve as the primary scientific liaison to pre\-training, post\-training, and product teams, ensuring that measurement insights directly influence model architecture and training recipes (the "evaluation flywheel")
* Mentorship \& Influence: Provide technical mentorship and guidance to senior research engineers and applied scientists, fostering a culture of scientific rigor and code without direct management responsibilities
* Hands\-on Contribution: Remain hands\-on in code and research, building prototypes for new evaluation frameworks and validating novel measurement theories

  

  

  

### **Minimum Qualifications:**

* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience
* Track record of driving technical strategy and landing large\-scale research or product impacts in a time\-sensitive environment
* Proven technical vision regarding the future trajectory of Generative AI, specifically in how model performance translates to user utility
* Expertise in designing and implementing online and offline measurement systems, benchmark building, and data synthesis techniques
* Experience leading complex, cross\-functional technical initiatives, driving consensus across engineering, research, and product boundaries
* Proficiency in Python and deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch), with the ability to prototype and implement complex methodologies

  

  

  

### **Preferred Qualifications:**

* PhD in Machine Learning, Statistics, Econometrics, Causal Inference, Computer Science, or related fields
* 8\+ years of experience, or PhD \+ 6 years of hands\-on experience in large language models, NLP, and Transformer modeling, within both research and engineering development settings
* Significant experience developing comprehensive evaluation frameworks for LLMs, multimodal models, or agentic systems
* Expertise in online measurement, user\-behavior modeling, counterfactual analysis, and A/B testing platform design
* Demonstrated background in AI system benchmarking, Reward modeling, RL, content evaluation, or human\-AI interaction
* Track record of publication in top\-tier conferences (NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, ACL) or significant contributions to open\-source AI ecosystems
* Ability to communicate complex scientific concepts clearly to executive leadership and non\-technical stakeholders to influence high\-level decision\-making
* Experience building and curating large\-scale datasets and ontologies used for training or evaluation

  

  

  

### **About Meta:**


Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.  

  

  

Meta is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disabil

roles.description:
**Overview**
------------


The Global Business Solutions Group (GBSG) Product Growth team is dedicated to accelerating business outcomes through a data\-driven, product\-led approach to growth. We leverage Product\-Led Growth strategies, scalable growth loops, and rigorous experimentation to identify and amplify the highest\-impact opportunities across the product ecosystem. Our team brings together exceptional talent across disciplines to run rapid experiments, conduct deep analytical investigations, and drive cross\-functional alignment that unlocks durable customer and revenue growth.

  

As a Sr. Staff Data Scientist, you will serve as a senior technical leader and strategic thought partner to product and business executives. You will shape the growth roadmap by framing ambiguous opportunities, defining measurable hypotheses, and leading the design and interpretation of complex experiments. You will elevate the analytical bar across the organization, applying advanced causal inference and statistical methodologies to separate signal from noise and guide high\-stakes investment decisions. Your work will directly influence company\-level growth strategy, ensuring we scale initiatives that create meaningful customer value and sustained top\-line impact.

 **Responsibilities**

**Set Technical Vision and Elevate the Analytical Bar**


* Shape and evolve the data science strategy that powers product\-led growth and scalable growth loops across the ecosystem.
* Define best practices for experimentation, causal inference, and advanced analytics to ensure high\-quality, decision\-grade insights.
* Apply sophisticated statistical modeling and machine learning techniques to transform complex, ambiguous data into clear strategic direction.


**Lead Deep Analytics and Experimentation at Scale**


* Develop a holistic view of the end\-to\-end customer journey, synthesizing signals across acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, and monetization.
* Frame high\-value hypotheses and design robust experiments that isolate causal impact and inform investment decisions.
* Partner with cross\-functional leaders to prioritize and scale the most impactful growth initiatives.


**Drive Cross\-Functional Influence and Execution**


* Act as a trusted thought partner to Product, Engineering, Design, Marketing, and Finance, translating analytical insights into strategic product and business actions.
* Influence roadmap decisions by grounding discussions in data, clear trade\-offs, and measurable outcomes.
* Raise the organization’s analytical maturity by improving instrumentation, data quality, logging standards, and experimentation frameworks.


**Serve as a Senior Technical Leader**


* Operate as a subject matter expert in causal inference and experimentation, mentoring other data scientists and setting a high standard for analytical rigor.
* Lead through influence, fostering a culture of hypothesis\-driven decision\-making and continuous learning.
* Tackle ambiguous, high\-stakes problems that span multiple teams and require both depth and breadth of expertise.


**Operate with Velocity and Impact**


* Rapidly synthesize diverse data sources and business metrics to uncover actionable insights.
* Balance methodological rigor with pragmatic execution, ensuring that insights are translated quickly into measurable business outcomes.

 **Qualifications**

**Experience and Technical Depth**


* 8\+ years of experience in data science, applied statistics, or advanced analytics, with a demonstrated track record of driving measurable product and business growth.
* Deep expertise in causal inference, experimentation, statistical modeling, and machine learning, with the ability to apply appropriate methodologies to complex, ambiguous problems.
* Proven ability to translate sophisticated analytical findings into clear, decision\-ready recommendations that influence strategy and investment.

**Technical Proficiency**


* Advanced proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language such as Python or R for large\-scale data analysis and modeling.
* Strong foundation in experimental design, hypothesis testing, and scalable analytics frameworks.
* Experience with data visualization tools and executive\-ready storytelling to communicate insights effectively.
* Familiarity with data architecture, instrumentation, and pipeline development to ensure high\-quality, reliable analytics is preferred.

**Leadership and Cross\-Functional Influence**


* Demonstrated ability to operate as a senior individual contributor who influences across Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Business leadership.
* Track record of navigating new domains quickly, identifying the critical metrics that matter, and establishing analytical clarity in ambiguous environments.
* Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to tailor messaging to technical and non\-technical audiences and drive alignment through data.

**Mindset and Impact Orientation**

roles.description:
**Overview**
------------


We are seeking a Senior Staff Data Scientist to join our Strategy Data Science team. This role is designed for a strategic, business\-oriented data scientist who thrives at the intersection of data, business strategy, and cross\-functional collaboration. You will partner with leaders across Product Marketing, Channel Marketing, Finance, Data Engineering, and other Data Science teams to translate evolving business strategies into impactful, data\-driven initiatives.


Your work will bridge the gap between raw data and high\-level business application, ensuring insights not only answer immediate questions but also guide long\-term strategic direction. You will apply deep product analytics, advanced analytical methods, modeling, and scenario planning to inform critical decisions and shape the future of Intuit’s growth.

  

You’ll thrive here if you are equally comfortable diving deep into data, navigating ambiguity, and influencing high\-level strategy.

 **Responsibilities**

* Set analytical direction to influence strategic roadmaps across QuickBooks customer growth initiatives by partnering with senior leaders in Product Marketing, Channel Marketing, Finance, Data Engineering, and Data Science to translate evolving business priorities into durable, data\-informed strategies and decision frameworks.
* Own and lead the most complex and ambiguous problem spaces, defining success metrics, analytical approaches, and learning agendas where the path forward is unclear and standard methodologies are insufficient.
* Invent and scale novel analytical frameworks and methodologies that synthesize experimentation, causal inference, research, product analytics, and financial modeling to uncover long\-term customer growth opportunities.
* Build and apply durable customer segmentation frameworks to improve product targeting, positioning, and customer experience.
* Serve as a strategic thought partner to executive and senior leadership, influencing roadmaps, investment decisions, and tradeoffs through clear articulation of insights, risks, limitations, and strategic implications.
* Multiply impact through mentorship and standards\-setting, raising the bar for analytical rigor and strategic storytelling across the data science and analytics community.

 **Qualifications**

* 10\+ years of experience in data science, strategy, or qualitative research roles. Experience in consulting or a strategy\-focused capacity is a plus.
* Technical proficiency in SQL, Python, GitHub, and Databricks. Experience with BI tools (Tableau, Qlik Sense) is a plus.
* Strong track record of hypothesis generation and translating insights into data\-driven execution with measurable impact on strategic roadmaps. Experience building models, analyzing experimentation, applying research, and using financial modeling to iterate on learning plans.
* Strong understanding of A/B testing and causal inference principles, with the ability to guide teams on interpretation, limitations, and strategic implications.
* Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to influence executives and collaborate effectively across technical and non\-technical teams.
* Ability to thrive in ambiguity and connect data to strategic clarity.

  

Intuit provides a competitive compensation package with a strong pay for performance rewards approach. This position may be eligible for a cash bonus, equity rewards and benefits, in accordance with our applicable plans and programs (see more about our compensation and benefits at Intuit®: Careers \| Benefits). Pay offered is based on factors such as job\-related knowledge, skills, experience, and work location. To drive ongoing fair pay for employees, Intuit conducts regular comparisons across categories of ethnicity and gender. The expected base pay range for this position is:


Bay Area California $204,500 \- $276,500
Southern California $197,500 \- $267,500

roles.title: Data Scientist, User Operations
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and AI relevance match—this role is built around experimentation, causal inference, and metrics design, perfectly aligned with your core expertise. The work is deeply impactful (human-AI collaboration at scale) and at a frontier company. Major concerns: (1) Seniority framing suggests individual contributor depth over player-coach/team leadership (explicit 'own' vs. lead), (2) Stage fit is diluted—OpenAI is Series C+ scale-up, not 0-to-1 Series B-D growth stage, and (3) Comp is likely $300-400K+ (levels.fyi data incomplete), which overshoots your target and may indicate overleveling or misalignment on role scope.
roles.description:
About the Team

OpenAI’s User Operations organization is building the data and intelligence layer behind AI-assisted operations — the systems that decide when automation should help users, when humans should step in, and how both improve over time. Our flagship platform is transforming customer support into a model for “agent-first” operations across OpenAI.

About the Role

As a Data Scientist on User Operations, you’ll design the models, metrics, and experimentation frameworks that power OpenAI’s human-AI collaboration loop. You’ll build systems that measure quality, optimize automation, and turn operational data into insights that improve product and user experience at scale. You’ll partner closely with Support Automation Engineering, Product, and Data Engineering to ensure our data systems are production-grade, trusted, and impactful.

This role is based in San Francisco or New York City. We use a hybrid work model of three days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees.

Why it matters

Every conversation users have with OpenAI products produces signals about how humans and AI interact. User Ops Data Science turns those signals into insights that shape how we support users today and design agentic systems for tomorrow. This is a unique opportunity to help define how AI collaboration at scale is measured and improved inside OpenAI.

In this role, you will:

 - Build and own metrics, classifiers, and data pipelines that determine automation eligibility, effectiveness, and guardrails.

 - Design and evaluate experiments that quantify the impact of automation and AI systems on user outcomes like resolution quality and satisfaction.

 - Develop predictive and statistical models that improve how OpenAI’s support systems automate, measure, and learn from user interactions.

 - Partner with engineering and product teams to create feedback loops that continuously improve our AI agents and knowledge systems.

 - Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for leadership and cross-functional stakeholders.

 - Develop and socialize dashboards, applications, and other ways of enabling the team and company to answer product data questions in a self-serve way

 - Contribute to establishing data science standards and best practices in an AI-native operations environment.

 - Partner with other data scientists across the company to share knowledge and continually synthesize learnings across the organization

You might thrive in this role if you have:

 - 10+ years of experience in data science roles within product or technology organizations.

 - Expertise in statistics and causal inference, applied in both experimentation and observational causal inference studies.

 - Expert-level SQL and proficiency in Python for analytics, modeling, and experimentation.

 - Proven experience designing and interpreting experiments and making statistically sound recommendations.

 - Experience building data systems or pipelines that power production workflows or ML-based decisioning.

 - Experience developing and extracting insights from business intelligence tools, such as Mode, Tableau, and Looker.

 - Strategic and impact-driven mindset, capable of translating complex business problems into actionable frameworks.

 - Ability to build relationships with diverse stakeholders and cultivate strong partnerships.

 - Strong communication skills, including the ability to bridge technical and non-technical stakeholders and collaborate across various functions to ensure business impact.

 - Ability to operate effectively in a fast-moving, ambiguous environment with limited structure.

 - Strong communication skills and the ability to translate complex data into stories for non-technical partners.

Nice-to-haves:

 - Familiarity with large language models or AI-assisted operations platforms.

 - Experience in operational automation or customer support analytics.

 - Background in experimentation infrastructure or human-AI interaction systems.

About OpenAI

OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity. 

We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic.

For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement https://cdn.openai.com/policies/eeo-policy-statement.pdf.
